quote_text: 挺纵不收
summary: 说明 priapism 时引用中医古籍中的病名术语。

quote_text: 强阳不倒
summary: 接《薛己医按》术语，列出《石室秘录》中的白话化说法。

quote_text: 阴茎坚长而精自出也
summary: 辨析强中与阴茎异常勃起时引古医书语。

quote_text: 常发虚阳，不交精出
summary: 继续解释强中的古医书用语。

quote_text:
别再杀飞蛾和蝴蝶了，
最后审判近在眼前了。
source_or_origin: 李敖译 William Blake《Auguries of Innocence》诗句
summary: 开场以布莱克诗句引出最后审判意象。

quote_text:
Kill not the Moth nor Butterfly，
For the Last Judgment draweth nigh.
summary: 同一布莱克诗句的英文原文。

quote_text: I shall never believe that God plays dice with the world.我绝不相信上帝会对世人掷骰子。
summary: 借“上帝不掷骰子”语意回应最后审判的宗教场景。

quote_text:
We Believe in Divine Judgment
God is not only the Creator but he is also the Judge of all the earth.
All men and nations stand before His judgment bar.
summary: 摘出关于上帝创造与审判的英文信条。

quote_text: 死后有报，纤毫受之
summary: 说明佛教也有死后审判观念时引用。

quote_text: 莫轻小恶，以为无罪
summary: 说明小恶亦会受审判时引用。

quote_text: 我又看见一个白色的大宝座，和坐在上面的那位……我又看见死了的人，无论大小，都站在宝座前。案卷都展开了，还有另一卷，就是生命册，也展开了。死了的人都凭着这些案卷所记载的，照着他们所行的受审判。（Then I saw a great white throne and him who was seated on it... And I saw the dead, great and small, standing before the throne, and books were opened. Then another book was opened, which is the book of life. And the dead were judged by what was written in the books, according to what they had done.）
summary: 上帝李背诵启示录白色大宝座审判段落。

quote_text: 父不审判人，却已经把审判的权柄完全交给子。
summary: 解释审判权柄交给子的经文。

quote_text: The Father judges no one，but has given all judgment to the Son.
summary: 同一《约翰福音》经文英译。

quote_text: 约翰不敢看坐在上面的，所以无从描述。
source_or_origin: 周联华牧师解释《启示录》
summary: 引用周联华牧师对启示录宝座上那位不可描述的解释。
notes: 校对补入：宗教注释，不属政治语录。

quote_text: 皇帝轮流做，今年到我家
summary: 借孙悟空名句说明轮值上帝的戏剧设定。

quote_text: Je me presse de rire de tout，de peur d'être obligé d'en pleurer.
summary: 用博马舍名言说明“快笑以防哭”。

quote_text: I quickly laugh at everything，for fear of having to cry.
summary: 同一博马舍名言的英文译句。

quote_text: 快笑以防哭
summary: 李敖概括博马舍名言的中文译法。

quote_text: Father，I cannot tell a lie，I did it with my little hatchet
source_or_origin: Mark Twain “Mark Twain as George Washington”
summary: 考证华盛顿砍樱桃树传说时列出马克吐温版本。

quote_text: I can't tell a lie，Pa；you know I can't tell a lie. I did cut it with my hatchet
source_or_origin: M. L. Weems《Life of George Washington》
summary: 列出威姆斯传记中砍樱桃树故事的版本。

quote_text: 当事实在你一边，辩论事实；当法律在你一边，辩论法律；两者都不在你一边，就大呼小叫。
summary: 以律师谚语形容辩论策略。

quote_text: When you have the facts on your side，argue the facts. When you have the law on your side，argue the law. When you have neither，holler.
summary: 同一律师谚语英文原文。

quote_text: 你可以欺骗所有人于暂时，你可以欺骗少数人于永久，但你不能欺骗所有人于永久
source_or_origin: You can fool all the people some of the time...
summary: 补收一条一般性欺骗格言；虽托名林肯，但非总统政策、演说或政治口号。
notes: 校对补入；保留为一般格言。

quote_text: You can fool all the people some of the time，and some of the people all the time，but you cannot fool all the people all of the time.
source_or_origin: 常见托名 Lincoln 的格言
summary: 同一欺骗格言的英文原文。
notes: 校对补入；保留为一般格言。

quote_text: Et maiores vestros et posteros cogitate.
summary: 指出小亚当斯演说句可溯源至塔西佗。

quote_text: Think of your forefathers and posterity.
summary: 同一塔西佗句子的英译。

quote_text: 想想你们的前辈和后代。
summary: 同一塔西佗句子的中文译法。

quote_text: 猫也可以一视国王
source_or_origin: A cat may look at a king
summary: 亚当斯以英谚说明卑微者也可直视权威。

quote_text: A cat may look at a king.
summary: 同一英谚原文。

quote_text: A cat may look at a Chinese God.
source_or_origin: 李敖化用 A cat may look at a king
summary: 把英谚改写进中国上帝的戏剧场景。

quote_text: 我觉得很难为情，你教育我们的青年人，而我们却盗印你的书。有朝一日，我们会有比较完善的法律，也许你们会采用我们的法律。
source_or_origin: R. W. Emerson 致 Thomas Carlyle 信
summary: 引用爱默生谈美国盗印英书时的歉意。

quote_text: 总该先给人家一点输入英国原版的时间。
source_or_origin: R. W. Emerson 致 Thomas Carlyle 信
summary: 补收爱默生同信中谈暂缓盗印的句子。
notes: 校对补入：版权书信语境，不属政治语录。

quote_text: 大哥莫话小弟
summary: 用来说明英国与美国在盗印问题上前科相似的俗语。

quote_text: You have undertaken to cheat me. I won't sue you, for the law is too slow. I'll ruin you.
summary: 引用范得比尔特关于法律太慢的强硬名言。

quote_text: 你耍老子，老子不告你，打官司太慢了，老子做掉你。
source_or_origin: 李敖译 Cornelius Vanderbilt 语
summary: 同一范得比尔特名言的中文译意。

quote_text: They have sown the wind, and they shall reap the whirlwind.

quote_text: 作恶之报，果大于因。
summary: 同一句圣经语的中文概括。

quote_text: There are cases of praise which could not be expected, and of reproach when the parties have been seeking to be perfect.
source_or_origin: 《孟子》“有不虞之誉，有求全之毁”英译
summary: 用孟子语说明林肯身后的赞毁。

quote_text: 有不虞之誉，有求全之毁
summary: 同一句孟子原文。

quote_text: Tom said to himself that it was not such a hollow world, after all. He had discovered a great law of human action, without knowing it-namely, that in order to make a man or a boy covet a thing, it is only necessary to make the thing difficult to attain. If he had been a great and wise philosopher, like the writer of this book, he would now have comprehended that Work consists of whatever a body is obliged to do and that Play consists of whatever a body is not obliged to do…（汤姆自言自语道：“世界可不是盖的。”他无意中对人类行为发现了一个伟大原则，这原则是没人知道的，就是：要一个人或一个小孩追求某件东西，唯一的妙计，就是使这件东西难以得到。假若他是一个伟大而聪明的哲学家——如本书作者，他一定知道工作是人人不得不做的，而游戏是人人可以不做的……）
source_or_origin: Mark Twain《The Adventures of Tom Sawyer》
summary: 引用汤姆索亚粉刷墙壁故事中关于工作与游戏的结论。

quote_text: 吃人不吐骨头

quote_text: If you pick us, do we not bleed ？If you tickle us ,do we not laugh？If you poison us, do we not die？ And if you wrong us , shall we not revenge？
source_or_origin: Shakespeare《The Merchant of Venice》Shylock 台词

quote_text: 你若刺我们一下，我们能不流血吗？你若搔我们的痒，我们能不笑吗？你若朝我们下毒，我们能不死吗？你若欺负我们，我们能不报仇吗？

summary: 同一莎士比亚台词的中文译意。

quote_text: Life is like a box of chocolates. You never know what you're gonna get.

summary: 引用汤誓中与日同亡的古典句。

quote_text: O sun，when wilt thou expire？We will die together with thee.
summary: 同一汤誓句的英文译法。

quote_text: 一朝被蛇咬，十年怕井绳。

quote_text: In contemporary Christian theology there is a growing tendency to describe hell as a state rather than a place.

summary: 同一神学引文的中文译意。

quote_text: L'Enfer，c'est les Autres. Hell is other people.

quote_text: 我又看见一个白色的大宝座，和坐在上面的那位。从他面前天地都逃避，再无可见之处了。我又看见死了的人，无论大小，都站在宝座前，案卷都展开了，还有另一卷，就是生命册，也展开了。死了的人都凭着这些案卷所记载的，照着他们所行的受审判。于是海交出其中的死人、死亡和阴间也交出其中的死人，他们都照各人所行的受审判。
summary: 终幕前再次引用白色大宝座审判段落。

quote_text:
Before the morning's dawn，the Eagle call'd the Vulture，
The Raven call'd the Hawk. I heard them from my forests，
Saying：“Let us go up far，for soon I smell upon the wind，
A terror coming from the South.”The Eagle and Hawk fled away
At dawn，and ere the sun arose，the Raven and Vulture follow'd.
Let us flee also to the North. They fled. The Sons of Men
Saw them depart in dismal droves. The trumpets sounded loud，
And all the Sons of Eternity descended into Beulah.
summary: 尾幕引用布莱克长诗《最后审判》的最后八行。

quote_text:
南风栗冽，闻风迫追。
众禽交语，吾侪北归。
日之出矣，无待熹微。
人子以目，号角鼓吹。
彼国永堕，来生成灰。
source_or_origin: 李敖译 William Blake《The Last Judgement》
summary: 同一布莱克诗段的中文译文。
